data.title: Home Bancorp (NASDAQ: HBCP) to report $1.39 EPS on Monday, 2026-01-26. Investors hopeful for positive results and guidance.
data.description: Home Bancorp (NASDAQ: HBCP) is set to reveal its quarterly earnings on Monday, January 26, 2026. Analysts project an earnings per share (EPS) of $1.39 for the company. The anticipation is high among investors as they await the announcement, with expectations of exceeding the predicted earnings and receiving positive guidance for the upcoming quarter. This information is crucial for potential new investors looking to make informed decisions. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: SPS PoolCare acquires Pool Troopers, solidifying #1 position in pool services. Expected to perform 2,000,000 weekly services by 2026 in 19 markets across 5 states.
data.description: SPS PoolCare has further strengthened its leading position in swimming pool services through the strategic acquisition of Pool Troopers. The combined company is projected to conduct over 2,000,000 weekly recurring pool services by 2026. Their service coverage extends across 19 markets in five states, encompassing three of the four largest markets in the region.

data.title: GlobalGPT mobile app launched on Google Play, offering an all-in-one AI platform accessible on phones worldwide.
data.description: On January 23, 2026, in Dover, Delaware, the GlobalGPT mobile application has been launched on Google Play. This app provides access to a robust all-in-one AI platform. By integrating with the web version, users can now enjoy a smooth AI experience on their phones and other devices. This launch signifies a major milestone in making advanced AI technology easily accessible, allowing users to engage with the platform conveniently.

data.title: Zip has been recognized as a Visionary in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Source-to-Pay Suites, standing out as the sole Agentic Procurement Orchestration Platform on the list.
data.description: In the recent Gartner Magic Quadrant for Source-to-Pay in 2026, Zip was recognized as a Visionary. It is noteworthy to mention that Zip is the sole agentic procurement orchestration platform to achieve this distinction. The recognition by Gartner indicates the company's high performance and innovative solutions in the industry.

data.title: NEACH & Braid to launch Fintech Integration Leadership Series in Spring 2026, offering financial institutions guidance.
data.description: In Burlington, Massachusetts, the New England Automated Clearing House Association (NEACH) revealed a collaboration with Braid to introduce the Fintech Integration Leadership Series. This national educational initiative aims to equip financial institutions with the essential structure to assess, introduce, and expand FI-hosted fintech and embedded payments programs efficiently and responsibly. The program is scheduled to kick off in March 2026, providing support to institutions seeking to navigate the landscape of fintech integration.

data.title: Mediacom Communications appoints Giuseppe B. Commisso as interim CEO. Giuseppe has been with the company for almost two decades.
data.description: Mediacom Communications, based in Mediacom Park, New York, has named Giuseppe B. Commisso as its Vice Chairman and interim Chief Executive Officer. Giuseppe, who has been with the company for nearly twenty years, previously served as Group Vice President of Corporate Finance and was a member of the Board of Directors. The appointment of Giuseppe came shortly before the passing of Rocco B. Commisso, marking a significant change in leadership for the company.

data.title: AI deployments increased in enterprises from pilots to mission-critical by 2025, leading to higher demand for cloud infrastructure and services - ISG.
data.description: According to a recent report by ISG, in 2025, enterprises have progressed from AI testing phases to implementing AI technologies for crucial operations. This shift has led to a rise in the need for updated cloud infrastructure and services in businesses.

data.title: Arden's Garden introduces Sea Moss Energy™ Wellness Shot in ATL, using Sea Moss Gel in innovative product.
data.description: An established cold-pressed juice and smoothie company is pushing the boundaries by introducing Sea Moss Gel as a key ingredient in their new wellness shots. Based in Atlanta, Arden's Garden has been a prominent player in the industry for three decades.

data.title: GFI Software names Zebra Systems as key North American distributor, enhancing growth in U.S. and Canada.
data.description: Strategic consolidation in the U.S. and Canada is boosting partner enablement and driving growth for GFI Software, a top player in AI-driven security and communication solutions for SMBs. Based in Austin, Texas, the company made this announcement on January 23, 2026.

data.title: Novartis CEO says $23 billion US investment will protect against potential tariffs, ensures supply from domestic facilities.
data.description: Novartis AG's CEO, Vas Narasimhan, expressed at the World Economic Forum in Davos that the Swiss drugmaker's recent $23 billion U.S. investment and an agreement with the U.S. government in December could shield it from potential tariffs imposed by the Trump administration. This move to expand the company's U.S. manufacturing capabilities allows Novartis to mainly serve the American market from domestic facilities. Narasimhan also mentioned that the existing stockpiles provide additional protection against any short-term trade interruptions. Trump's threats of tariffs were not overlooked in the discussions.

data.title: Folger Consort to perform 15th-century French love songs at Folger Shakespeare Library on Valentine's Day Weekend.
data.description: In early 2026, Folger Consort, a renowned early music ensemble at the Folger Shakespeare Library, is offering audiences the chance to experience the enchanting sounds of Love Songs from the 15th Century. This romantic program will be showcased in the Elizabethan-style Theatre starting in February. Be prepared to be serenaded by timeless melodies from this era.
